summary: PR 2737 split the former `melee/ft/ft_0892.c` range into `ft_0892.c`, new `ft_0899.c`, and new `ft_08A1.c`, updated GALE01 splits/configure status accordingly, moved db sound prototypes into a new `dbsound.h`, and made small symbol/header/library adjustments. The bot report shows net linked-code and data improvement, with functions removed from the old unit reappearing as matches in the new split units, while `ft_0899:ft_80089B08` remained 98.98% and `lbBgFlash_80020E38` had a small unmatched regression.

key_files.role: Adjusted `ft_0892.c` text/sdata2 ranges and introduced split entries for `ft_0899.c` and `ft_08A1.c`.
Changed build object status: `ft_0892.c` and `ft_08A1.c` marked Matching, new `ft_0899.c` marked NonMatching.
Trimmed to the earlier range by removing later functions and no-longer-needed includes/local declarations.
New split unit containing `fn_8008998C`, `ft_80089B08`, `ft_8008A1B8`, local `ftData_x58_t`, and dependencies for IK/floor/flash behavior.
New split unit for later fighter common transition/input helpers, with multiple matched functions reported by the bot.
New header for sound-info debug functions and `db_804D4AF8`; prototypes were removed from `db.h` and included from `dbinit.c`.
Adjusted symbol metadata for `lbArq_804316C0` size and `gm_80479D30` size/scope.
Small data-related change; bot report says `main/melee/it/itcoll` `.sdata2` reached 100%.
Small library change associated with bot-reported `main/melee/lb/lbarq` `.bss` reaching 100%.
Small header adjustment paired with lbarq/symbol metadata changes.
One-line library change; bot report notes a one-byte unmatched regression in `lbBgFlash_80020E38`.

what_changed: Split `melee/ft/ft_0892.c` at `0x8008998C` and `0x8008A1FC`, creating `melee/ft/ft_0899.c` and `melee/ft/ft_08A1.c` in `splits.txt`.
Updated `configure.py` so `ft_0892.c` became Matching, new `ft_0899.c` remained NonMatching, and new `ft_08A1.c` became Matching.
Moved sound-info declarations out of `db.h` into new `src/melee/db/dbsound.h`, including `fn_SetupSoundInfo`, `fn_UpdateSoundInfo`, `fn_CheckSoundInfo`, and extern `db_804D4AF8`.
Moved later functions out of `ft_0892.c`; the new split units carry the relevant includes and local declarations needed for their extracted ranges.
Adjusted GALE01 symbol metadata for `lbArq_804316C0` and `gm_80479D30`.
Touched small support files in `itcoll`, `lbarq`, and `lbbgflash`; the loaded diff is truncated for those hunks, but the bot report records related data/match effects.
smart_moves: The split was reflected consistently across `splits.txt`, `configure.py`, and source files, avoiding a source-only move without build/split metadata.
Header ownership was narrowed by creating `dbsound.h` instead of keeping sound debug declarations in the broader `db.h`.
The PR preserved address-style and unknown names such as `fn_8008998C`, `ftData_x58_t`, and `db_804D4AF8` where the slice does not show semantic naming evidence.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When splitting a partially matched translation unit, update split ranges and object matching status together so moved functions are attributed to the new units rather than appearing as lost matches in the old unit.

A bot report for a split PR may show apparent broken matches in the old unit and new matches in the new units for the same functions; interpret those as relocation effects when the function names and byte counts line up.
If a newly split unit needs declarations from a narrower subsystem, a dedicated header can reduce broad-header coupling, as shown by moving sound-info declarations from `db.h` into `dbsound.h`.

naming_conventions.convention: Unproven functions and globals kept address-style names such as `fn_8008998C`, `fn_SetupSoundInfo`, and `db_804D4AF8` rather than receiving speculative semantic names.
New split unit filenames follow address-range naming (`ft_0899.c`, `ft_08A1.c`) matching their GALE01 split start addresses.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Address-range split: `ft_0892.c` was shortened, and the extracted ranges were assigned to new units so already-matching functions could be linked in their correct objects.

The new `ft_0899.c` retains stack/codegen-sensitive constructs visible in the diff, including temporary byte padding locals, a `volatile f32 line_len_sqrt`, `(void)&line_len_sqrt`, and `PAD_STACK(16)` in `ft_80089B08`; the bot report shows that function improved to 98.98% but not a full match.
Symbol metadata edits for `.bss` sizes/scope accompanied data matching changes, with bot-reported new data matches in `lbarq`, `gm_1A3F`, and related units.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.summary: GALE01 report showed linked code +2384 bytes (+0.06%), matched data +680 bytes (+0.06%), linked data +24 bytes, 14 new matches, 8 broken matches in old `ft_0892` entries corresponding to moved functions/data, one unmatched-item improvement for `ft_0899:ft_80089B08` to 98.98%, and two unmatched-item regressions including `lbBgFlash_80020E38` by one byte.

follow_up_queries: ft_0892 split ft_0899 ft_08A1 moved matches

notes: PR body is empty and there are no human review comments in the loaded slice.
The raw diff is truncated, so small hunks for `itcoll`, `lbarq`, and `lbbgflash` are known mainly from changed-file metadata and the bot report rather than full inline code.
Bot report provides matching/data deltas, but no build log, objdiff detail, or author explanation is loaded.
Because `ft_0899.c` is explicitly NonMatching and `ft_80089B08` is 98.98%, retained stack/codegen tactics should not be treated as accepted style without later evidence.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Interpret split-PR broken matches as relocation when new-unit matches line up
accepted_candidate_records.text: In a TU split, bot reports can list moved functions as broken in the old unit and new matches in the new unit. Compare function names and byte counts before treating them as true regressions.

source_update_candidates.title: `ft_0892` was split into `ft_0892`, `ft_0899`, and `ft_08A1` in PR 2737
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2737 changed GALE01 splits so `melee/ft/ft_0892.c` covers text `0x800892D4..0x8008998C`, new `melee/ft/ft_0899.c` covers `0x8008998C..0x8008A1FC`, and new `melee/ft/ft_08A1.c` covers `0x8008A1FC..0x8008A494`; configure marks `ft_0892.c` and `ft_08A1.c` Matching and `ft_0899.c` NonMatching.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path-scoped history for future work on the split fighter range.

source_update_candidates.title: `dbsound.h` introduced for debug sound-info declarations
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2737 created `src/melee/db/dbsound.h` for `fn_SetupSoundInfo`, `fn_UpdateSoundInfo`, `fn_CheckSoundInfo`, and `db_804D4AF8`, removed the function prototypes from `db.h`, and included `dbsound.h` from `dbinit.c`.
source_update_candidates.reason: Header ownership context may help future prototype/include cleanup.

source_update_candidates.title: `ft_0899:ft_80089B08` remained just short of matching after split
source_update_candidates.text: The PR's bot report lists `main/melee/ft/ft_0899` `ft_80089B08` as an unmatched-item improvement from 0.00% to 98.98%; the diff shows stack/codegen-sensitive constructs including `volatile f32 line_len_sqrt`, `(void)&line_len_sqrt`, and `PAD_STACK(16)` in the moved function.
source_update_candidates.reason: Future cleanup should know these tactics were present in a nonmatching function and were not proven as final style by this slice.

rejection_notes: Do not promote `PAD_STACK(16)`, volatile local lifetime steering, or byte padding locals from this PR as accepted global tactics; the loaded evidence shows the function remained 98.98% and NonMatching.
Do not infer human reviewer approval or rationale; no human reviews or review comments are present in the loaded slice.
Do not treat the one-byte `lbBgFlash_80020E38` regression as intentionally accepted beyond the fact that the PR was merged; the slice lacks author or reviewer explanation.
